task_completion.description: Open NASA Panoply and load the NCEP precipitation dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply application window is open, and the dataset 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' is visible in the sources or datasets panel.

task_completion.description: Generate and export a 1D time-series line plot of precipitation at 9N, 80W.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 1D line plot window in Panoply showing precipitation over time for coordinates 9N, 80W, or a file manager/image viewer showing the exported 'annual_precip_profile.png'.

task_completion.description: Generate and export a geo-mapped spatial plot zoomed on Central America for March.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D map plot window in Panoply zoomed in on Central America for the month of March (Time = 3 or similar indicator), or the exported 'panama_precip_march.png' visible in a file manager/image viewer.

task_completion.description: Generate and export a geo-mapped spatial plot zoomed on Central America for October.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D map plot window in Panoply zoomed in on Central America for the month of October (Time = 10 or similar indicator), or the exported 'panama_precip_october.png' visible in a file manager/image viewer.

task_completion.description: Create a report identifying the critical months and associated risks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing 'draft_restriction_report.txt' with content identifying March as a HIGH risk month (drought/low precipitation) and October as a LOW risk month (wet/high precipitation).

integrity.description: The agent used the NASA Panoply GUI to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with Panoply's plot creation windows, adjusting map projections, or using the 'Export Image' dialog, rather than bypassing the GUI.

integrity.description: The generated plots appear to be genuine Panoply outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported PNGs should feature standard Panoply visual elements, such as its distinct color bars, map borders, and dataset metadata subtitles, rather than generic matplotlib or internet-sourced images.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a terminal script to automatically generate the outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show the execution of a Python or shell script that directly creates the .png or .txt files without GUI interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: March is the critical drought month with the lowest precipitation, resulting in a HIGH draft restriction risk. October is a heavy precipitation month, resulting in a LOW draft restriction risk. The expected output files are: annual_precip_profile.png, panama_precip_march.png, panama_precip_october.png, and draft_restriction_report.txt.
